---
format: gfm
title: "`dplyr` updates & table overview guidelines"
author: "Beth Jump"
date: "2026-04-02"
execute: 
  warning: false
message: false
---
  
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

## Updates from Posit

You can sign up for regular updates from Posit [here](https://posit.co/about/subscription-management/?mkt_tok=NzA5LU5YTi03MDYAAAGgU2gr6j8rcpq7fjFRPZOq_hAieCw4XGBl5nMreIkmR3OT_j46oWFS5i5-CTFTD0a8GDmNFzp7UPxOeyBwgwDamJq1hxpl24epKuo46PEfmA). Each month they send out a `posit::glimpse()` newsletter that I think is really useful.

From the February newsletter...

## There are some new updates to the `dplyr` package!

The full overview is [here](https://tidyverse.org/blog/2026/02/dplyr-1-2-0/). The main changes:

1. `filter_out()` is a new function to complement `filter()`
2. `when_any()` and `when_all()` are new helper functions that join `all_of()` and `any_of()` to work with vectors
3. `recode_values()`, `replace_values()`, and `replace_when()` are here to make your `case_when()` recoding sections less verbose

## Future meeting: comparing tables

I want to put together a high level overview of packages we use to make tables. The overview will include when to use each package (HTML, PDF, Word, etc) and examples of how to make a table. 

Packages:

- flextable
- GT
- kable
- tinytable 

Formats:

Each table:
